title: Tiniaria aubertii

Tiniaria aubertii is a type of shrub or small tree that is known for its attractive foliage and fragrant flowers. The plant is often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes. Its classification is further detailed in the APG III system, which groups it within the clade Asterids.

intro:
There is no plant called "Tiniaria aubertii." However, I found a plant called "Tilia aubertii", also known as Silver Linden or Silver European Lime. 

Tilia aubertii is a deciduous tree species in the family Malvaceae, native to China. The health benefits of Tilia aubertii include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Tilia aubertii include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tilia aubertii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
